calls.note: IPO valuations attractive; AMC business is strong with recurring income, low default risk and growing equity penetration; Indian AUM penetration low vs US/Singapore/Hong Kong. Strong SBI brand and reach (though SBI Cards disappointed). Every investor with moderate-to-high risk appetite can apply with a longer-term view; listing gains possible if the market is positive.

Grasim subsidiary buying Shell's renewable energy business for Rs 17,200 crore. Deal momentum brings the green/renewable energy space - subdued and consolidating for a while - back into the limelight. Good to focus on Aditya Birla Group shares today.

Shares have been consolidating a long time due to family disputes over more than Rs 1 lakh crore of assets. Supreme Court CJI's bench has directed mediation through a middleman; if any resolution comes there is a chance of good activity in the group. Keep in focus.

Street gave a thumbs-up to results; whole IT pack positive and deal wins starting. As a contrarian long-term view, valuations are attractive - for the first time in history HCL Tech trades below a PE of 20. Slowly accumulate on dips; chance of a 20-30% rally from here. For the short term keep a trailing stop-loss.

Defence company with a strong order book but visible execution challenges; last two quarters flattish and some management/corporate-governance issues. Will underperform in the medium-to-short term; hold with a one-year-plus horizon as valuations are attractive. Book profit if it reaches the 280-300 range.

Order book is very strong and building; stock is a bit moderate and recently in a consolidation phase. Hold if the investor has a one-to-two-year time horizon. This quarter's results are very crucial for the company.
Asked whether it could be held for a three-to-six-month view; he said hold at present, without a target.

Exit on rallies. Hit a ~20% upper circuit on the renewables rally and the Aditya Birla-Shell deal could bring some further rally, but last two years show clear underperformance. Now under Reliance Industries, and buying interest is not coming in these associated group companies. Hold only on an extremely long-term valuation view - otherwise other stocks will outperform.

Hold existing positions; wait for results. Negative AI sentiment on IT is being neutralised and valuations are attractive - IT's 20-year average PE is ~30 but almost every stock now trades below 20 PE. Overhangs remain and an indication is needed; suits patient long-term investors. In the medium-to-short term other stocks have a clear chance of outperforming.

Hold for now and watch this quarter's results; the stock is coming into the limelight again after six months to a year of consolidation. If the results are in any way disappointing, exit on rallies and go into Vedanta Power.

Stock is in consolidation after good outperformance two years back. New value-added products (Heritage ice creams) introduced last quarter give a chance of margins rising. It is a multi-dimensional business, unlike the standalone ice-cream business of Quality Walls.

Has fully bought the Netherlands' Magnum business from Hindustan Unilever and is now Magnum's holding company; strong performance in the stock. But it is by and large a standalone ice-cream business, versus multi-dimensional Heritage - switch from Heritage only if you have the risk appetite.
If the caller wants Vedanta Power, build a separate position in it rather than switching out. If Suzlon's results this quarter disappoint, exit Suzlon on rallies and move into Vedanta Power.

Definitely hold for the long term, and accumulate in this price range for a long-term view. Recent OFS was subscribed by very strong companies though some underperformance is visible. The medium-to-short-term overhang on all PSUs is that the government sells stake at a discount as soon as the stock performs, to clear its deficit.

Hold. Consolidating at 1,300 (down from 1,400) after global brokerage downgrades. For a long-term investor, once earnings recovery comes there is a chance of upgrades again - keep 1,900 as the medium-to-long-term target. If it reaches the 1,750 range in the medium-to-short term, book profit.
Hold. Positivity is coming into the banking sector. The stock has corrected from around 326 to the 250 range and looks attractive here for the medium to long term.
Hold, along with Bank of Baroda, on the back of returning positivity in the banking sector.

Hold for the medium to long term; wait for this quarter's results. A moderate correction is on, with the quarter's business updates impacting the stock negatively, but a rebound is possible from this range. All Tata Group shares are consolidating. Discretionary spending is rising and the Zudio brand is a key revenue generator with growing tier-one/tier-two penetration.

A Maruti associated group company that has rallied from almost 100 - a good company to buy on dips after seeing this quarter's results. Enter based on how the quarterly results come, not on the price movement. A strict stop-loss is a must for any trading, since the stock has risen 100% in just one-two months and these stocks tend to rally then go into long consolidation.

Hold at this price range and watch this quarter's results; not for a fresh entry. The stock is very strong and resilient but is seeing profit booking after a recent all-time high of 2,600. Strong support at Rs 2,000 - keep that as a trailing stop-loss and hold the position.
